data.content:
The file is generated successfully but never added to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time (before targets run), so the generated file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation and track for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's glob for `*.cs` files is evaluated at project evaluation time (before any targets run), so files created during the build won't be automatically picked up.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, after the file is generated. Let me also add it to `FileWrites` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me use the skill for this since it's specifically about generated files not being compiled.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation and track for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    </ItemGroup>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. 

**Root cause:** MSBuild globs (`**/*.cs`) expand at evaluation time — before any targets run. The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the file but never told MSBuild to compile it.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>` so the generated file is included in compilation and cleaned properly.
